title: vriesea × leopoldii
intro: Vriesea × leopoldii, also known as leopoldia or tiger's jaw, is a hybrid species of air plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is renowned for its unique appearance, characterized by its bright yellow flowers and long, pointed leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and sore throats, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The leaves of Vriesea × leopoldii can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as teas and infusions, which have been found to promote respiratory health, boost the immune system, and provide relief from digestive issues.
